prompts: Nuclear fission is the splitting of heavy atomic nuclei into smaller nuclei, releasing considerable energy, often used in nuclear reactors. In contrast, nuclear fusion involves the merging of light atomic nuclei into a heavier nucleus, releasing even more energy than fission, and is the process powering the sun. Fission requires neutron bombardment to initiate, while fusion needs extreme heat and pressure. Fission produces highly radioactive waste, whereas fusion generates minimal long-lived radioactive waste. Energy from fusion is still under research for controlled use, while fission is a mature technology with established applications.
